];

export function createAIRouter({ backrest, backrestHetzner, ssh }) {
  const router = Router();

  // Process tool calls from AI
  async function executeTool(name, args) {
    switch (name) {
      case "list_snapshots":
        return await backrest.listSnapshots("do-spaces-backup", args.plan_id);

      case "browse_snapshot_files":
        return await backrest.listSnapshotFiles("do-spaces-backup", args.snapshot_id, args.path);

      case "check_backup_status": {
        const results = { local: null, hetzner: null };
        try { results.local = await backrest.getOperations(); } catch (e) { results.local = { error: e.message }; }
        try { results.hetzner = backrestHetzner ? await backrestHetzner.getOperations() : { error: "Not configured" }; } catch (e) { results.hetzner = { error: e.message }; }
        return results;
      }

      case "check_server_status":
        try {
          const result = await ssh.execute('docker ps --format "table {{.Names}}\\t{{.Status}}\\t{{.Image}}"');
          return { reachable: true, containers: result.stdout };
        } catch (e) {
          return { reachable: false, error: e.message };
        }

      case "check_container_status":
        try {
          const result = await ssh.execute(`docker inspect --format '{{json .State}}' ${args.container_name}`);
          return JSON.parse(result.stdout);
        } catch (e) {
          return { error: e.message };
        }

      case "check_container_logs":
        try {
          const lines = args.lines || 50;
          const result = await ssh.execute(`docker logs --tail ${lines} ${args.container_name} 2>&1`);
          return { logs: result.stdout || result.stderr };
        } catch (e) {
          return { error: e.message };
        }

      case "propose_restore_files":
        return {
          type: "action_proposal",
          action: "restore_files",
          params: { repo: "do-spaces-backup", snapshotId: args.snapshot_id, sourcePath: args.source_path, targetPath: args.target_path },
          description: args.description,
          warning: `This will overwrite files at ${args.target_path} on the target server.`,
        };

      case "propose_restore_database":
        return {
          type: "action_proposal",
          action: "restore_database",
          params: { snapshotId: args.snapshot_id, dumpPath: args.dump_path, containerName: args.container_name, user: args.db_user || "postgres", database: args.database_name || "" },
          description: args.description,
          warning: "This will overwrite the current database contents with the backup data.",
        };

      case "propose_restart_container":
        return {
          type: "action_proposal",
          action: "restart_container",
          params: { containerName: args.container_name },
          description: args.description,
        };

      case "propose_ssh_command":
        return {
          type: "action_proposal",
          action: "ssh_command",
          params: { command: args.command },
          description: args.description,
          warning: "This will run a shell command on the production server.",
        };

      case "list_docker_volumes":
        try {
          const result = await ssh.execute("docker volume ls --format '{{.Name}}' && echo '---' && docker system df -v --format '{{.Name}}\\t{{.Size}}' 2>/dev/null || true");
          return { volumes: result.stdout };
        } catch (e) {
          return { error: e.message };
        }

      case "diff_snapshots":
        try {
          const cmd = args.path
            ? `diff --path ${args.path} ${args.snapshot_id_a} ${args.snapshot_id_b}`
            : `diff ${args.snapshot_id_a} ${args.snapshot_id_b}`;
          return await backrest.runCommand("do-spaces-backup", cmd);
        } catch (e) {
          return { error: e.message };
        }

      default:
        return { error: `Unknown tool: ${name}` };
    }
  }

  router.post("/", async (req, res) => {
    const { messages, model } = req.body;

    const openai = new OpenAI({
      baseURL: "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
      apiKey: process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY,
    });

    const selectedModel = model || process.env.DEFAULT_MODEL || "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4";

    // Build conversation with system prompt
    const fullMessages = [{ role: "system", content: SYSTEM_PROMPT }, ...messages];

    try {
      // Loop to handle tool calls
      let response = await openai.chat.completions.create({
        model: selectedModel,
        messages: fullMessages,
        tools: TOOLS,
        max_tokens: 4096,
      });

      let message = response.choices[0].message;
      const allMessages = [...fullMessages, message];
      const toolResults = [];

      // Process tool calls (up to 10 rounds)
      let rounds = 0;
      while (message.tool_calls && message.tool_calls.length > 0 && rounds < 10) {
        rounds++;
        for (const toolCall of message.tool_calls) {
          const args = JSON.parse(toolCall.function.arguments || "{}");
          const result = await executeTool(toolCall.function.name, args);
          const toolMessage = {
            role: "tool",
            tool_call_id: toolCall.id,
            content: JSON.stringify(result),
          };
          allMessages.push(toolMessage);

          // If it's a proposal, include it in the response for the UI to render
          if (result?.type === "action_proposal") {
            toolResults.push(result);
          }
        }

        response = await openai.chat.completions.create({
          model: selectedModel,
          messages: allMessages,
          tools: TOOLS,
          max_tokens: 4096,
        });
        message = response.choices[0].message;
        allMessages.push(message);
      }

      res.json({
        message: message.content,
        proposals: toolResults,
        model: selectedModel,
        usage: response.usage,
      });
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("AI chat error:", err);
      res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
    }
  });

  return router;
}
��������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������src/backrest.js�������������������������������������������������������������������������������������0000664�0000000�0000000�00000016402�15166042467�0014017�0����������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������ustar�00root����������������������������root����������������������������0000000�0000000������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������/**
 * Backrest API client using ConnectRPC (HTTP POST + JSON)
 * Backrest uses ConnectRPC, so all calls are POST to /v1.Backrest/{Method}
 *
 * This module provides two layers:
 * 1. Raw client (createBackrestClient) — thin wrapper around the HTTP API
 * 2. Normalized adapters — transform upstream responses into stable internal schemas
 *    so the frontend never breaks when Backrest changes field names or envelope shapes.
 */

// ─── Normalization helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Normalize a single snapshot object into a stable internal schema.
 * Handles various field-name drifts from upstream Backrest.
 */
function normalizeSnapshot(raw) {
  return {
    id: raw.id || raw.snapshotId || raw.SnapshotId || "",
    // Timestamp: prefer explicit epoch-ms fields, fall back to time strings
    snapshotTimestamp: raw.snapshotTimestamp || raw.unixTimeMs || raw.timeMs || raw.startTimeMs || null,
    // Tags / plan identification
    tags: Array.isArray(raw.tags) ? raw.tags : raw.planId ? [`plan:${raw.planId}`] : [],
    // Size info
    summaryStats: raw.summaryStats || raw.stats || null,
    totalBytesProcessed:
      raw.summaryStats?.totalBytesProcessed ||
      raw.stats?.totalBytesProcessed ||
      raw.totalBytesProcessed ||
      raw.size ||
      null,
    // Paths
    paths: raw.paths || raw.pathList || raw.path || [],
    // Host / metadata
    hostname: raw.hostname || raw.host || raw.machineName || null,
    // Original for escape-hatch
    _raw: raw,
  };
}

/**
 * Normalize an operation object into a stable internal schema.
 */
function normalizeOperation(raw) {
  return {
    id: raw.id || raw.operationId || raw.OperationId || "",
    status: raw.status || raw.operationStatus || raw.Status || "",
    type: raw.op || raw.type || raw.operationType || raw.Op || "",
    planId: raw.planId || raw.plan || "",
    repoId: raw.repoId || raw.repo || "",
    // Timestamps
    unixTimeStartMs: raw.unixTimeStartMs || raw.startTimeMs || raw.timeMs || raw.startedAt || null,
    unixTimeEndMs: raw.unixTimeEndMs || raw.endTimeMs || raw.finishedAt || null,
    // Display
    displayMessage: raw.displayMessage || raw.message || raw.errorMessage || "",
    // Size / progress
    totalBytesProcessed: raw.totalBytesProcessed || raw.stats?.totalBytesProcessed || null,
    // Original for escape-hatch
    _raw: raw,
  };
}

/**
 * Normalize a repo/config object into a stable internal schema.
 */
function normalizeRepo(raw) {
  return {
    id: raw.id || raw.repoId || "",
    name: raw.name || raw.displayName || raw.id || "",
    storageType: raw.storageType || raw.storage || raw.type || "",
    status: raw.status || raw.state || "",
    // Original for escape-hatch
    _raw: raw,
  };
}

/**
 * Normalize the dashboard summary into a stable internal schema.
 */
function normalizeDashboard(raw) {
  return {
    totalSnapshots: raw.totalSnapshots || raw.snapshotCount || raw.snapshots?.length || 0,
    totalSize: raw.totalSize || raw.totalBytes || raw.repoSize || null,
    lastOperation: raw.lastOperation ? normalizeOperation(raw.lastOperation) : null,
    repos: Array.isArray(raw.repos) ? raw.repos.map(normalizeRepo) : [],
    // Original for escape-hatch
    _raw: raw,
  };
}

// ─── Raw client ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export function createBackrestClient(baseUrl) {
  async function call(method, body = {}) {
    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/v1.Backrest/${method}`, {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify(body),
    });
    if (!res.ok) {
      const text = await res.text();
      throw new Error(`Backrest ${method} failed (${res.status}): ${text}`);
    }
    return res.json();
  }

  return {
    // Dashboard & status
    async getDashboard() {
      return call("GetSummaryDashboard", {});
    },

    // Config
    async getConfig() {
      return call("GetConfig", {});
    },

    // Snapshots
    async listSnapshots(repoId, planId) {
      const body = { repoId };
      if (planId) body.planId = planId;
      return call("ListSnapshots", body);
    },

    // Browse files in a snapshot
    async listSnapshotFiles(repoId, snapshotId, path) {
      return call("ListSnapshotFiles", { repoId, snapshotId, path });
    },

    // Trigger a backup
    async backup(planId) {
      return call("Backup", { value: planId });
    },

    // Restore files from a snapshot
    async restore(repoId, snapshotId, sourcePath, targetPath) {
      return call("Restore", { repoId, snapshotId, path: sourcePath, target: targetPath });
    },

    // Operations history
    async getOperations(lastN) {
      return call("GetOperations", { lastN: lastN || 50 });
    },

    // Repo health
    async doRepoTask(repoId, task) {
      return call("DoRepoTask", { repoId, task });
    },

    // Run arbitrary restic command
    async runCommand(repoId, command) {
      return call("RunCommand", { repoId, command });
    },

    // ─── Normalized adapters ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

    /** Get dashboard with normalized response shape */
    async getDashboardNormalized() {
      const raw = await call("GetSummaryDashboard", {});
      return normalizeDashboard(raw);
    },

    /** Get config with normalized repos */
    async getConfigNormalized() {
      const raw = await call("GetConfig", {});
      return {
        repos: Array.isArray(raw.repos) ? raw.repos.map(normalizeRepo) : [],
        _raw: raw,
      };
    },

    /** List snapshots with normalized response shape */
    async listSnapshotsNormalized(repoId, planId) {
      const raw = await this.listSnapshots(repoId, planId);
      const items = extractArray(raw);
      return items.map(normalizeSnapshot);
    },

    /** Get operations with normalized response shape */
    async getOperationsNormalized(lastN) {
      const raw = await call("GetOperations", { lastN: lastN || 50 });
      const items = extractArray(raw);
      return items.map(normalizeOperation);
    },
  };
}

// ─── Utility: extract array from various response envelopes ────────────────────

function extractArray(data) {
  if (Array.isArray(data)) return data;
  if (data && typeof data === "object") {
    if (Array.isArray(data.snapshots)) return data.snapshots;
    if (Array.isArray(data.items)) return data.items;
    if (Array.isArray(data.results)) return data.results;
    if (Array.isArray(data.data)) return data.data;
    if (Array.isArray(data.operations)) return data.operations;
  }
  return [];
}

// ─── Export normalization functions for server-side use ────────────────────────

export { normalizeSnapshot, normalizeOperation, normalizeRepo, normalizeDashboard, extractArray };
��������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������src/server.js���������������������������������������������������������������������������������������0000664�0000000�0000000�00000050414�15166042467�0013530�0����������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������ustar�00root����������������������������root����������������������������0000000�0000000������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������import express from "express";
import session from "express-session";
import passport from "passport";
import { Strategy as GoogleStrategy } from "passport-google-oauth20";
import { Strategy as LocalStrategy } from "passport-local";
import bcrypt from "bcrypt";
import { config } from "dotenv";
import { fileURLToPath } from "url";
import { dirname, join } from "path";
import fs from "fs/promises";
import { createAIRouter } from "./ai.js";
import { createBackrestClient, extractArray, normalizeSnapshot, normalizeOperation } from "./backrest.js";
import { createSSHClient } from "./ssh.js";

config();

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
const app = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

// ─── Audit log helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Lightweight file-based audit trail for restore proposals and approvals.
// Stored as newline-delimited JSON for easy tailing and grep.

const AUDIT_LOG_PATH = join(__dirname, "../data/audit.log.jsonl");

async function ensureAuditDir() {
  try {
    await fs.mkdir(join(__dirname, "../data"), { recursive: true });
  } catch {
    // directory may already exist
  }
}

async function auditLog(entry) {
  await ensureAuditDir();
  const line = JSON.stringify({
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    ...entry,
  }) + "\n";
  await fs.appendFile(AUDIT_LOG_PATH, line, "utf8");
}

// ─── Last-known-good health cache ──────────────────────────────────────────────
// Prevents the dashboard from showing misleading zeros when upstream APIs
// are temporarily unreachable.

const HEALTH_CACHE_PATH = join(__dirname, "../data/health-cache.json");
let healthCache = {
  lastSuccessfulFetch: null,
  data: null,
  warning: null, // set when live fetch fails but cache is served
};

async function loadHealthCache() {
  try {
    const raw = await fs.readFile(HEALTH_CACHE_PATH, "utf8");
    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
    healthCache = { ...healthCache, ...parsed };
  } catch {
    // no cache file yet — that's fine
  }
}

async function saveHealthCache(data) {
  await ensureAuditDir();
  healthCache = {
    lastSuccessfulFetch: new Date().toISOString(),
    data,
    warning: null,
  };
  await fs.writeFile(HEALTH_CACHE_PATH, JSON.stringify(healthCache, null, 2), "utf8");
}

async function markHealthCacheStale(warning) {
  healthCache.warning = warning;
  // persist the warning so a restart still shows it
  await ensureAuditDir();
  await fs.writeFile(HEALTH_CACHE_PATH, JSON.stringify(healthCache, null, 2), "utf8");
}

// ─── Health aggregation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Backend-computed health model. The frontend should consume this single endpoint
// instead of deriving status from ad-hoc inference.

const KNOWN_PLANS = ["coolify-config", "docker-volumes", "db-dumps"];
const STALENESS_THRESHOLD_MS = 25 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 25 hours

async function computeHealth() {
  const health = {
    overall: "unknown",
    by_plan: {},
    ssh_reachable: null,
    local_backrest_reachable: false,
    hetzner_backrest_reachable: false,
    repository_read_test: null,
    last_computed: new Date().toISOString(),
  };

  // Initialize per-plan buckets
  for (const plan of KNOWN_PLANS) {
    health.by_plan[plan] = {
      status: "unknown",
      latest_snapshot_count: 0,
      last_success: null,
      last_failure: null,
      consecutive_failures: 0,
      freshness_age_ms: null,
    };
  }

  // 1. Check local Backrest reachability
  try {
    const config = await backrest.getConfig();
    health.local_backrest_reachable = true;
    const repos = config.repos || [];
    health.repository_read_test = repos.length > 0 ? "ok" : "empty";
  } catch (err) {
    health.local_backrest_reachable = false;
    health.repository_read_test = "error";
  }

  // 2. Check Hetzner Backrest reachability
  if (backrestHetzner) {
    try {
      await backrestHetzner.getConfig();
      health.hetzner_backrest_reachable = true;
    } catch {
      health.hetzner_backrest_reachable = false;
    }
  }

  // 3. Check SSH reachability
  try {
    const sshResult = await ssh.execute("echo ok");
    health.ssh_reachable = (sshResult.stdout || "").trim() === "ok";
  } catch {
    health.ssh_reachable = false;
  }

  // 4. Fetch snapshots and compute per-plan health
  try {
    const repo = "do-spaces-backup";
    const rawSnapshots = await backrest.listSnapshots(repo);
    const snapshots = extractArray(rawSnapshots).map(normalizeSnapshot);

    // Count snapshots per plan
    for (const snap of snapshots) {
      const plan = planFromTags(snap.tags);
      if (!health.by_plan[plan]) {
        health.by_plan[plan] = {
          status: "unknown",
          latest_snapshot_count: 0,
          last_success: null,
          last_failure: null,
          consecutive_failures: 0,
          freshness_age_ms: null,
        };
      }
      health.by_plan[plan].latest_snapshot_count++;
      const ts = snap.snapshotTimestamp;
      if (ts) {
        if (!health.by_plan[plan].last_success || ts > health.by_plan[plan].last_success) {
          health.by_plan[plan].last_success = ts;
        }
        const age = Date.now() - (typeof ts === "string" ? parseInt(ts) : ts);
        if (health.by_plan[plan].freshness_age_ms === null || age < health.by_plan[plan].freshness_age_ms) {
          health.by_plan[plan].freshness_age_ms = age;
        }
      }
    }

    // Determine per-plan status
    for (const [plan, info] of Object.entries(health.by_plan)) {
      if (info.latest_snapshot_count === 0) {
        info.status = "no_snapshots";
      } else if (info.freshness_age_ms !== null && info.freshness_age_ms > STALENESS_THRESHOLD_MS) {
        info.status = "stale";
      } else {
        info.status = "healthy";
      }
    }
  } catch (err) {
    // Snapshot fetch failed — mark all plans as unreachable
    for (const plan of Object.keys(health.by_plan)) {
      health.by_plan[plan].status = "unreachable";
      health.by_plan[plan].consecutive_failures++;
    }
  }

  // 5. Fetch recent operations to detect failures
  try {
    const rawOps = await backrest.getOperations(20);
    const ops = extractArray(rawOps).map(normalizeOperation);
    for (const op of ops) {
      const plan = op.planId || "unknown";
      if (!health.by_plan[plan]) continue;
      if (op.status === "STATUS_ERROR") {
        health.by_plan[plan].last_failure = op.unixTimeStartMs;
        health.by_plan[plan].consecutive_failures++;
      } else if (op.status === "STATUS_SUCCESS") {
        health.by_plan[plan].consecutive_failures = 0;
      }
    }
  } catch {
    // operations fetch failed — non-fatal
  }

  // 6. Compute overall status
  const planStatuses = Object.values(health.by_plan).map((p) => p.status);
  if (planStatuses.every((s) => s === "healthy")) {
    health.overall = "healthy";
  } else if (planStatuses.some((s) => s === "unreachable" || s === "error")) {
    health.overall = "degraded";
  } else if (planStatuses.some((s) => s === "stale")) {
    health.overall = "stale";
  } else if (planStatuses.every((s) => s === "no_snapshots")) {
    health.overall = "empty";
  } else {
    health.overall = "degraded";
  }

  return health;
}

/** Extract plan name from snapshot tags (mirrors frontend logic) */
function planFromTags(tags) {
  if (!tags || !Array.isArray(tags)) return "unknown";
  if (tags.some((t) => t.includes("coolify-config"))) return "coolify-config";
  if (tags.some((t) => t.includes("docker-volumes"))) return "docker-volumes";
  if (tags.some((t) => t.includes("db-dumps"))) return "db-dumps";
  return "unknown";
}

// ─── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
app.use(express.json());
app.use(express.static(join(__dirname, "../public")));
app.use(
  session({
    secret: process.env.SESSION_SECRET || "change-me",
    resave: false,
    saveUninitialized: false,
    cookie: { secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production", maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 },
  })
);

// ─── Google OAuth ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
const allowedEmails = (process.env.ALLOWED_EMAILS || "")
  .split(",")
  .map((e) => e.trim().toLowerCase());

passport.use(
  new GoogleStrategy(
    {
      clientID: process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
      clientSecret: process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET,
      callbackURL: process.env.CALLBACK_URL,
    },
    (accessToken, refreshToken, profile, done) => {
      const email = profile.emails?.[0]?.value?.toLowerCase();
      if (allowedEmails.includes(email)) {
        return done(null, { id: profile.id, email, name: profile.displayName });
      }
      return done(null, false, { message: "Unauthorized email" });
    }
  )
);

// Local fallback auth (backup if Google SSO is unavailable)
passport.use(
  new LocalStrategy(async (username, password, done) => {
    const expectedUser = process.env.BACKUP_USERNAME || "admin";
    const passwordHash = process.env.BACKUP_PASSWORD_HASH;
    if (!passwordHash) return done(null, false, { message: "Local auth not configured" });
    if (username !== expectedUser) return done(null, false, { message: "Invalid credentials" });
    const match = await bcrypt.compare(password, passwordHash);
    if (!match) return done(null, false, { message: "Invalid credentials" });
    return done(null, { id: "local", email: username, name: "Local Admin" });
  })
);

passport.serializeUser((user, done) => done(null, user));
passport.deserializeUser((user, done) => done(null, user));

app.use(passport.initialize());
app.use(passport.session());

// Auth routes — Google SSO
app.get("/auth/google", passport.authenticate("google", { scope: ["profile", "email"] }));
app.get(
  "/auth/google/callback",
  passport.authenticate("google", { failureRedirect: "/login?error=unauthorized" }),
  (req, res) => res.redirect("/")
);
app.get("/auth/logout", (req, res) => {
  req.logout(() => res.redirect("/login"));
});
app.get("/auth/me", (req, res) => {
  if (req.isAuthenticated()) return res.json(req.user);
  res.status(401).json({ error: "Not authenticated" });
});
app.get("/login", (req, res) => {
  res.sendFile(join(__dirname, "../public/login.html"));
});

// Local auth fallback — POST /auth/local
app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: false }));
app.post(
  "/auth/local",
  passport.authenticate("local", { failureRedirect: "/login?error=invalid" }),
  (req, res) => res.redirect("/")
);

// Auth middleware for API routes
function requireAuth(req, res, next) {
  if (req.isAuthenticated()) return next();
  res.status(401).json({ error: "Not authenticated" });
}

// ─── Initialize clients ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
const backrest = createBackrestClient(process.env.BACKREST_URL);
const backrestHetzner = process.env.BACKREST_HETZNER_URL
  ? createBackrestClient(process.env.BACKREST_HETZNER_URL)
  : null;
const ssh = createSSHClient();

// Track Hetzner Backrest connectivity
let hetznerReachable = null; // null = unknown, true/false = cached result
async function checkHetznerReachable() {
  if (!backrestHetzner) return false;
  try {
    await backrestHetzner.getConfig();
    hetznerReachable = true;
  } catch {
    hetznerReachable = false;
  }
  return hetznerReachable;
}
// Run initial check (non-blocking)
checkHetznerReachable().catch(() => {});

// Load health cache on startup
loadHealthCache().catch(() => {});

// ─── API routes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * GET /api/health
 * Returns the backend-computed health model.
 * Falls back to cached last-known-good if live computation fails.
 */
app.get("/api/health", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const health = await computeHealth();
    // Save successful computation to cache
    await saveHealthCache(health);
    res.json(health);
  } catch (err) {
    // Serve stale cache if available
    if (healthCache.data) {
      await markHealthCacheStale(`Live health check failed: ${err.message}`);
      res.json({
        ...healthCache.data,
        overall: "degraded",
        cache_warning: healthCache.warning,
        last_computed: healthCache.data.last_computed,
      });
    } else {
      res.status(500).json({ error: err.message, overall: "unknown" });
    }
  }
});

app.get("/api/status", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const [localStatus, hetznerStatus] = await Promise.allSettled([
      backrest.getDashboard(),
      backrestHetzner?.getDashboard(),
    ]);
    res.json({
      local: localStatus.status === "fulfilled" ? localStatus.value : { error: localStatus.reason?.message },
      hetzner: !backrestHetzner
        ? { notConfigured: true }
        : hetznerStatus?.status === "fulfilled"
          ? { ...hetznerStatus.value, reachable: true }
          : { error: hetznerStatus?.reason?.message, reachable: hetznerReachable },
    });
  } catch (err) {
    res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

app.get("/api/repos", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const config = await backrest.getConfig();
    const repos = (config.repos || []).map(r => ({ id: r.id, name: r.name || r.id }));
    res.json({ repos });
  } catch (err) {
    res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

app.get("/api/snapshots", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const snapshots = await backrest.listSnapshots(req.query.repo || "do-spaces-backup");
    res.json(snapshots);
  } catch (err) {
    res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

app.get("/api/snapshots/:id/files", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const files = await backrest.listSnapshotFiles(
      req.query.repo || "do-spaces-backup",
      req.params.id,
      req.query.path || "/"
    );
    res.json(files);
  } catch (err) {
    res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

app.get("/api/operations", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const ops = await backrest.getOperations();
    res.json(ops);
  } catch (err) {
    res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

app.get("/api/containers", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const result = await ssh.execute(
      'docker ps --format \'{"name":"{{.Names}}","status":"{{.Status}}","image":"{{.Image}}","id":"{{.ID}}"}\' | head -50'
    );
    const lines = (result.stdout || "").trim().split("\n").filter(Boolean);
    const containers = lines.map((line) => {
      try { return JSON.parse(line); } catch { return null; }
    }).filter(Boolean);
    res.json({ containers });
  } catch (err) {
    res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

app.get("/api/models", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const response = await fetch("https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models/user", {
      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY}` },
    });
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`OpenRouter returned ${response.status}`);
    const data = await response.json();
    const available = (data.data || []).map((m) => ({
      id: m.id,
      name: m.name || m.id,
      description: m.description || "",
    }));
    res.json({
      default: process.env.DEFAULT_MODEL || "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
      available,
    });
  } catch (err) {
    // Fall back to a minimal hardcoded list if the API call fails
    res.json({
      default: process.env.DEFAULT_MODEL || "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4",
      available: [
        { id: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4", name: "Claude Sonnet 4" },
        { id: "anthropic/claude-opus-4", name: "Claude Opus 4" },
        { id: "anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5", name: "Claude Haiku 4.5" },
      ],
      error: err.message,
    });
  }
});

// ─── Audit log read endpoint ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
app.get("/api/audit", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const raw = await fs.readFile(AUDIT_LOG_PATH, "utf8");
    const lines = raw.trim().split("\n").filter(Boolean);
    const entries = lines.map((line) => {
      try { return JSON.parse(line); } catch { return null; }
    }).filter(Boolean);
    // Return most recent first, limit to 100
    res.json({ entries: entries.reverse().slice(0, 100) });
  } catch {
    res.json({ entries: [] });
  }
});

// ─── AI chat endpoint ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
const aiRouter = createAIRouter({ backrest, backrestHetzner, ssh });
app.use("/api/chat", requireAuth, aiRouter);

// ─── Execute approved action (with audit logging) ──────────────────────────────
app.post("/api/execute", requireAuth, async (req, res) => {
  const { action, params } = req.body;
  const user = req.user;

  try {
    // Audit: log the proposal/attempt
    await auditLog({
      event: "action_proposed",
      action,
      params: sanitizeParamsForAudit(params),
      user: user ? { id: user.id, email: user.email } : null,
    });

    let result;
    switch (action) {
      case "restore_files":
        result = await backrest.restore(params.repo, params.snapshotId, params.sourcePath, params.targetPath);
        break;
      case "ssh_command":
        result = await ssh.execute(params.command);
        break;
      case "restart_container":
        result = await ssh.execute(`docker restart ${params.containerName}`);
        break;
      case "restore_database":
        result = await ssh.execute(
          `docker exec -i ${params.containerName} psql -U ${params.user || "postgres"} ${params.database || ""} < ${params.dumpPath}`
        );
        break;
      default:
        return res.status(400).json({ error: `Unknown action: ${action}` });
    }

    // Audit: log the successful execution
    await auditLog({
      event: "action_executed",
      action,
      params: sanitizeParamsForAudit(params),
      user: user ? { id: user.id, email: user.email } : null,
      success: true,
    });

    res.json({ success: true, result });
  } catch (err) {
    // Audit: log the failure
    await auditLog({
      event: "action_failed",
      action,
      params: sanitizeParamsForAudit(params),
      user: user ? { id: user.id, email: user.email } : null,
      error: err.message,
    });

    res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

/** Strip sensitive or overly large fields from audit params */
function sanitizeParamsForAudit(params) {
  if (!params || typeof params !== "object") return params;
  const sanitized = { ...params };
  // Truncate very long strings to keep audit log manageable
  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(sanitized)) {
    if (typeof value === "string" && value.length > 500) {
      sanitized[key] = value.slice(0, 500) + "...[truncated]";
    }
  }
  return sanitized;
}

// ─── Start ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
app.listen(PORT, "0.0.0.0", () => {
  console.log(`Restore Wizard running on port ${PORT}`);
});
����������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������src/ssh.js������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������0000664�0000000�0000000�00000021741�15166042467�0013020�0����������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������ustar�00root����������������������������root����������������������������0000000�0000000������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������/**
 * SSH client for executing commands on the Hetzner production server
 * Used during restore operations
 *
 * Hardening improvements:
 * - Explicit timeout handling on all commands
 * - Allowlisted operation templates for common actions
 * - Safe quoting helpers to prevent injection
 * - stderr/code normalization
 * - Per-action audit logging (server-side)
 */

import { NodeSSH } from "node-ssh";

// ─── Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000; // 30 seconds
const MAX_COMMAND_LENGTH = 4096;

// ─── Allowlisted operation templates ───────────────────────────────────────────
// Instead of accepting arbitrary shell commands, these templates define
// safe, parameterized commands for common restore actions.

const ALLOWED_OPERATIONS = {
  /** List running containers */
  list_containers: {
    template: () =>
      'docker ps --format \'{"name":"{{.Names}}","status":"{{.Status}}","image":"{{.Image}}","id":"{{.ID}}"}\' | head -50',
    description: "List running Docker containers",
  },

  /** Restart a specific container */
  restart_container: {
    template: (params) => `docker restart ${safeShellArg(params.containerName)}`,
    description: "Restart a Docker container",
    validate: (params) => {
      if (!params.containerName || !/^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$/.test(params.containerName)) {
        throw new Error("Invalid container name");
      }
    },
  },

  /** Check if a container is running */
  check_container: {
    template: (params) => `docker inspect --format '{{.State.Running}}' ${safeShellArg(params.containerName)}`,
    description: "Check if a container is running",
    validate: (params) => {
      if (!params.containerName || !/^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$/.test(params.containerName)) {
        throw new Error("Invalid container name");
      }
    },
  },

  /** Test connectivity */
  ping: {
    template: () => "echo ok",
    description: "Test SSH connectivity",
  },

  /** Check disk space */
  disk_space: {
    template: () => "df -h / | tail -1",
    description: "Check disk space",
  },

  /** List recent Docker events */
  docker_events: {
    template: () => "docker events --since 1h --until now --format '{{.Time}} {{.Type}} {{.Action}}' | tail -20",
    description: "Show recent Docker events",
  },
};

// ─── Safe quoting ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Escape a value for safe use as a shell argument.
 * Wraps in single quotes and escapes any embedded single quotes.
 */
function safeShellArg(value) {
  const s = String(value);
  if (s.length > 256) {
    throw new Error("Argument too long for safe shell escaping");
  }
  // Only allow alphanumeric, dash, underscore, dot, slash for paths
  if (/^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$/.test(s)) {
    return s;
  }
  // Otherwise wrap in single quotes, escaping embedded single quotes
  return "'" + s.replace(/'/g, "'\\''") + "'";
}

// ─── Timeout wrapper ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Execute a command with a timeout. NodeSSH doesn't have built-in timeout
 * for execCommand, so we race against a Promise.
 */
async function execWithTimeout(ssh, command, timeoutMs = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
  let timeoutId;
  const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) => {
    timeoutId = setTimeout(() => {
      reject(new Error(`Command timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms: ${command.slice(0, 100)}`));
    }, timeoutMs);
  });

  const execPromise = ssh.execCommand(command, { cwd: "/" });

  try {
    const result = await Promise.race([execPromise, timeoutPromise]);
    clearTimeout(timeoutId);
    return result;
  } catch (err) {
    clearTimeout(timeoutId);
    throw err;
  }
}

// ─── Connection management ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Create and return a connected SSH client.
 * Validates that required environment variables are set.
 */
async function getConnection() {
  const host = process.env.HETZNER_SSH_HOST;
  const keyPath = process.env.HETZNER_SSH_KEY_PATH;

  if (!host) {
    throw new Error("HETZNER_SSH_HOST environment variable is not set");
  }
  if (!keyPath) {
    throw new Error("HETZNER_SSH_KEY_PATH environment variable is not set");
  }

  const ssh = new NodeSSH();
  await ssh.connect({
    host,
    port: parseInt(process.env.HETZNER_SSH_PORT || "22", 10),
    username: process.env.HETZNER_SSH_USER || "root",
    privateKeyPath: keyPath,
    // Connection-level timeout
    readyTimeout: 10_000,
  });
  return ssh;
}

// ─── Public API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export function createSSHClient() {
  return {
    /**
     * Execute a raw command (legacy path — use executeOperation when possible).
     * Includes timeout and length validation.
     */
    async execute(command, timeoutMs = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
      if (!command || typeof command !== "string") {
        throw new Error("Command must be a non-empty string");
      }
      if (command.length > MAX_COMMAND_LENGTH) {
        throw new Error(`Command exceeds maximum length of ${MAX_COMMAND_LENGTH} characters`);
      }

      const ssh = await getConnection();
      try {
        const result = await execWithTimeout(ssh, command, timeoutMs);
        return normalizeResult(result, command);
      } finally {
        ssh.dispose();
      }
    },

    /**
     * Execute an allowlisted operation by name with validated parameters.
     * This is the preferred path for known actions.
     *
     * @param {string} operationName - Key from ALLOWED_OPERATIONS
     * @param {object} params - Parameters for the operation template
     * @param {number} timeoutMs - Optional timeout override
     */
    async executeOperation(operationName, params = {}, timeoutMs = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
      const op = ALLOWED_OPERATIONS[operationName];
      if (!op) {
        throw new Error(`Unknown operation: ${operationName}. Allowed: ${Object.keys(ALLOWED_OPERATIONS).join(", ")}`);
      }

      // Validate params if the operation defines a validator
      if (op.validate) {
        op.validate(params);
      }

      const command = op.template(params);
      const ssh = await getConnection();
      try {
        const result = await execWithTimeout(ssh, command, timeoutMs);
        return normalizeResult(result, command);
      } finally {
        ssh.dispose();
      }
    },

    /**
     * Execute multiple commands sequentially, stopping on first failure.
     * Includes per-command timeout.
     */
    async executeMultiple(commands, timeoutMs = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
      const ssh = await getConnection();
      const results = [];
      try {
        for (const cmd of commands) {
          if (cmd.length > MAX_COMMAND_LENGTH) {
            results.push({
              command: cmd,
              stdout: "",
              stderr: `Command exceeds maximum length of ${MAX_COMMAND_LENGTH} characters`,
              code: -1,
              timedOut: false,
            });
            break;
          }
          const result = await execWithTimeout(ssh, cmd, timeoutMs);
          const normalized = normalizeResult(result, cmd);
          results.push(normalized);
          if (result.code !== 0) break;
        }
        return results;
      } finally {
        ssh.dispose();
      }
    },

    /** Expose the allowlist for documentation/UI purposes */
    getAllowedOperations() {
      return Object.entries(ALLOWED_OPERATIONS).map(([name, op]) => ({
        name,
        description: op.description,
      }));
    },
  };
}

// ─── Result normalization ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Normalize SSH execution results into a consistent shape.
 * Ensures stdout/stderr are always strings and code is always a number.
 */
function normalizeResult(result, command) {
  return {
    stdout: typeof result.stdout === "string" ? result.stdout : "",
    stderr: typeof result.stderr === "string" ? result.stderr : "",
    code: typeof result.code === "number" ? result.code : -1,
    command: command,
    timedOut: false,
  };
}

 * Lightweight validation tests for backrest-wiz modules.
 * Run: node src/test.js
 */

import {
  normalizeSnapshot,
  normalizeOperation,
  normalizeRepo,
  normalizeDashboard,
  extractArray,
} from "./backrest.js";

let passed = 0;
let failed = 0;

function assert(condition, label) {
  if (condition) {
    passed++;
    console.log(`  ✓ ${label}`);
  } else {
    failed++;
    console.error(`  ✗ ${label}`);
  }
}

// ─── extractArray tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
console.log("\n[extractArray]");
assert(Array.isArray(extractArray([])), "returns empty array for []");
assert(extractArray([]).length === 0, "empty array has length 0");
assert(
  extractArray({ snapshots: [1, 2] }).length === 2,
  "extracts from .snapshots"
);
assert(
  extractArray({ items: [1] }).length === 1,
  "extracts from .items"
);
assert(
  extractArray({ data: [1, 2, 3] }).length === 3,
  "extracts from .data"
);
assert(
  extractArray({ operations: [1] }).length === 1,
  "extracts from .operations"
);
assert(
  extractArray({ results: [1, 2] }).length === 2,
  "extracts from .results"
);
assert(
  extractArray({ foo: "bar" }).length === 0,
  "returns [] for non-matching object"
);
assert(
  extractArray(null).length === 0,
  "returns [] for null"
);

// ─── normalizeSnapshot tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
console.log("\n[normalizeSnapshot]");
const snap1 = normalizeSnapshot({
  id: "abc123",
  snapshotTimestamp: 1700000000000,
  tags: ["plan:coolify-config"],
  summaryStats: { totalBytesProcessed: 1024 },
});
assert(snap1.id === "abc123", "preserves id");
assert(snap1.snapshotTimestamp === 1700000000000, "preserves timestamp");
assert(Array.isArray(snap1.tags), "tags is array");
assert(snap1.totalBytesProcessed === 1024, "extracts size from summaryStats");

const snap2 = normalizeSnapshot({
  snapshotId: "xyz",
  unixTimeMs: 1600000000000,
  stats: { totalBytesProcessed: 2048 },
});
assert(snap2.id === "xyz", "falls back to snapshotId");
assert(snap2.snapshotTimestamp === 1600000000000, "falls back to unixTimeMs");
assert(snap2.totalBytesProcessed === 2048, "falls back to stats.totalBytesProcessed");

const snap3 = normalizeSnapshot({});
assert(snap3.id === "", "empty id defaults to empty string");
assert(snap3.snapshotTimestamp === null, "empty timestamp defaults to null");

// ─── normalizeOperation tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
console.log("\n[normalizeOperation]");
const op1 = normalizeOperation({
  id: "op1",
  status: "STATUS_SUCCESS",
  op: "OP_BACKUP",
  planId: "coolify-config",
  unixTimeStartMs: 1700000000000,
  displayMessage: "backup completed",
});
assert(op1.id === "op1", "preserves id");
assert(op1.status === "STATUS_SUCCESS", "preserves status");
assert(op1.type === "OP_BACKUP", "preserves type");
assert(op1.planId === "coolify-config", "preserves planId");
assert(op1.displayMessage === "backup completed", "preserves displayMessage");

const op2 = normalizeOperation({
  operationId: "op2",
  operationStatus: "STATUS_ERROR",
  operationType: "OP_RESTORE",
  message: "restore failed",
});
assert(op2.id === "op2", "falls back to operationId");
assert(op2.status === "STATUS_ERROR", "falls back to operationStatus");
assert(op2.displayMessage === "restore failed", "falls back to message");

// ─── normalizeRepo tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
console.log("\n[normalizeRepo]");
const repo1 = normalizeRepo({
  id: "repo1",
  name: "My Repo",
  storageType: "s3",
});
assert(repo1.id === "repo1", "preserves id");
assert(repo1.name === "My Repo", "preserves name");
assert(repo1.storageType === "s3", "preserves storageType");

const repo2 = normalizeRepo({ repoId: "r2", displayName: "Display" });
assert(repo2.id === "r2", "falls back to repoId");
assert(repo2.name === "Display", "falls back to displayName");

// ─── normalizeDashboard tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
console.log("\n[normalizeDashboard]");
const dash1 = normalizeDashboard({
  totalSnapshots: 42,
  totalSize: 999999,
  lastOperation: {
    id: "last",
    status: "STATUS_SUCCESS",
    op: "OP_BACKUP",
  },
  repos: [{ id: "r1", name: "Repo1" }],
});
assert(dash1.totalSnapshots === 42, "preserves totalSnapshots");
assert(dash1.totalSize === 999999, "preserves totalSize");
assert(dash1.lastOperation !== null, "normalizes lastOperation");
assert(dash1.lastOperation.id === "last", "lastOperation has correct id");
assert(dash1.repos.length === 1, "normalizes repos");
assert(dash1.repos[0].id === "r1", "repo has correct id");

const dash2 = normalizeDashboard({});
assert(dash2.totalSnapshots === 0, "empty dashboard has 0 snapshots");
assert(dash2.lastOperation === null, "empty dashboard has no lastOperation");

// ─── SSH safeShellArg tests (inline since it's not exported) ───────────────────
console.log("\n[SSH safeShellArg — inline validation]");
// We can't import the function directly since it's not exported, but we can
// validate the regex pattern used for validation
const safePattern = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$/;
assert(safePattern.test("coolify-db"), "alphanumeric + dash passes");
assert(safePattern.test("/data/coolify/app"), "path with slashes passes");
assert(safePattern.test("my_container_1"), "underscore passes");
assert(!safePattern.test("foo; rm -rf /"), "injection attempt fails");
assert(!safePattern.test("$(whoami)"), "command substitution fails");
assert(!safePattern.test("foo'bar"), "single quote fails");

// ─── Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
console.log(`\n${"=".repeat(50)}`);
console.log(`Results: ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed`);
console.log("=".repeat(50));

if (failed > 0) {
  process.exit(1);
} else {
  console.log("\nAll tests passed ✓");
  process.exit(0);

# Architecture

## Topology

```text
Browser
  → Cloudflare Tunnel (backup.designflow.app)
  → Restore Wizard  ← DO droplet, this repo
      → local Backrest API (DO droplet, port 9898, read-only)
      → SSH → Hetzner VPS (allowlisted commands only)
      → SSH → Authentik server (ubuntu@43.173.75.208)
      → SSH → Compshop server (root@185.194.148.230)
      → OpenRouter API (AI assistant)

Hetzner VPS (primary producer)
  → Backrest runtime → encrypted restic snapshots → DO Spaces /backrest
  → pre-backup hook → SQL + SQLite + Redis artifacts → backed up in db-dumps plan

Authentik server (secondary producer)
  → Backrest runtime → encrypted restic snapshots → DO Spaces /backrest/authentik
  → pre-backup hook → SQL + Redis artifacts

Compshop server (secondary producer)
  → Backrest runtime → encrypted restic snapshots → DO Spaces /backrest/compshop
  → pre-backup hook → SQL + Redis artifacts

DO Spaces
  → three separate restic repositories, all in the same bucket
```

### SSH-proxied Backrest API

For Authentik and Compshop, the Restore Wizard cannot reach their Backrest APIs directly (no Tailscale, no public port). Instead it SSHs into each server and proxies `curl localhost:9898` requests through the SSH session. The `sshBackrestCall()` helper in `server.js` implements this: it builds a `curl` command, executes it via SSH, and parses the JSON response with the existing normalization functions. This means Authentik/Compshop snapshot data flows through the same code path as the Hetzner Backrest API data.

### Key invariant

Monitor health ≠ backup health. All three servers run Backrest on their own schedules, independent of this app. If the DO droplet or the Restore Wizard goes down, backups continue uninterrupted on all servers. `computeHealth()` tracks SSH reachability for each server separately (`ssh_reachable`, `authentik_reachable`, `compshop_reachable`) and surfaces degraded-but-operational states explicitly.

## Components

### `src/server.js`

Express app. Central orchestration point.

- **`computeHealth()`** — backend health model. Checks per-plan snapshot freshness, SSH reachability to all three servers, local Backrest API reachability, and optional Hetzner Backrest API reachability. Returns a structured payload including `ssh_reachable`, `authentik_reachable`, `compshop_reachable`, and per-plan buckets.
- **`sshBackrestCall(sshClient, method, body)`** — proxies a Backrest ConnectRPC call through SSH by running `curl localhost:9898` on the remote server. Used to query Authentik and Compshop Backrest APIs without exposing any ports.
- **`sshListContainers(sshClient)`** / **`sshListSnapshots(sshClient)`** — helpers that use `sshBackrestCall` / raw `execute` to gather per-server data.
- **Health cache** — `saveHealthCache()` / `loadHealthCache()`. On health computation failure, `/api/health` returns the last-good cached state with a staleness warning.
- **`auditLog()`** — appends newline-delimited JSON to `AUDIT_LOG_PATH` for every propose/execute/fail event. Exposed at `/api/audit`.
- **Auth** — Google OAuth primary (`GoogleStrategy`), local username/password fallback (`LocalStrategy`). Fallback exists so operators retain access if Google SSO is unavailable.
- **API routes** — `/api/health`, `/api/status`, `/api/repos`, `/api/snapshots`, `/api/snapshots/:id/files`, `/api/snapshots/authentik`, `/api/snapshots/compshop`, `/api/operations`, `/api/containers`, `/api/containers/authentik`, `/api/containers/compshop`, `/api/models`, `/api/audit`, `/api/chat`, `/api/execute`, `/api/hiclaw/status`, `/api/restore/hiclaw`.

### `src/backrest.js`

ConnectRPC HTTP client + normalization layer.

Backrest uses ConnectRPC over HTTP POST — all calls are `POST /v1.Backrest/{Method}` with JSON bodies. Upstream field names drift across Backrest versions. Raw responses are transformed through normalization functions into a stable internal schema before any other module touches them:

- `normalizeSnapshot()` — stable snapshot shape with `id`, `snapshotTimestamp`, `tags`, `totalBytesProcessed`, `paths`, `hostname`
- `normalizeOperation()` — stable operation shape
- `normalizeRepo()` — stable repo shape
- `normalizeDashboard()` — stable dashboard shape
- `extractArray()` — handles 6 different response envelope shapes observed in the wild

This is intentional defensive engineering. The browser UI still contains some defensive fallback field access as a transition artifact — both layers are independently correct and the overlap is harmless.

### `src/ssh.js`

SSH execution layer. `createSSHClient(cfg)` accepts an optional config object (`{ host, user, keyPath, port }`) so multiple server clients can share the same key file. Server.js instantiates three clients: `ssh` (Hetzner), `sshAuthentik`, `sshCompshop`.

**Allowlisted operations** (all routes for Hetzner; raw `execute()` used for Authentik/Compshop):

| Operation | Command |
|---|---|
| `list_containers` | `docker ps` |
| `restart_container` | `docker restart <name>` |
| `check_container` | `docker inspect <name>` |
| `ping` | `echo ok` |
| `disk_space` | `df -h` |
| `docker_events` | `docker events --since 1h` |
| `hiclaw_status` | `docker inspect` on hiclaw-controller + hiclaw-manager |
| `restore_hiclaw` | `/opt/backrest/scripts/restore-hiclaw.sh` (10-min timeout) |

`safeShellArg()` sanitizes all interpolated arguments. `execWithTimeout()` races execution against a configured timeout. `normalizeResult()` returns a consistent `{ stdout, stderr, code }` shape regardless of outcome.

A raw `execute()` method is retained for legacy compatibility and is also used by `sshBackrestCall()` in `server.js` to proxy Backrest API requests to Authentik/Compshop.

### `src/ai.js`

OpenRouter (OpenAI-compatible) chat router. Implements a tool loop (up to 10 rounds) over 12 defined tools:

`list_snapshots`, `browse_snapshot_files`, `check_backup_status`, `check_server_status`, `check_container_status`, `check_container_logs`, `propose_restore_files`, `propose_restore_database`, `propose_restart_container`, `propose_ssh_command`, `list_docker_volumes`, `diff_snapshots`

Destructive proposals — file restore, database restore, container restart — are returned to the UI as proposal objects for explicit operator approval. The AI never executes destructive actions directly.

The system prompt embeds the full infrastructure topology, application list, database map, restore levels A–E, IP change checklist, and behavioral rules.

### `public/index.html`

Single-file SPA with embedded CSS and JS. No build step, no bundler — intentional for deployment simplicity. The app checks auth at startup, loads data in parallel, and polls health every 60s and snapshots every 90s.

Key subsystems: dashboard (`renderDashboard`), snapshot browser with file browsing modal (`openFileBrowser`), restore modal (`openRestoreModal`, `confirmRestore`), AI chat tab with proposal approval (`approveProposal`, `denyProposal`).

### `src/test.js`

45 inline assertions, no test framework. Covers `extractArray`, all four normalization functions, and SSH `safeShellArg` patterns. Exits with code 1 on any failure. Runs in under a second with no network or running services required.

## Backup plans (all three servers)

All three servers use the same three-plan structure:

| Plan | Path | Schedule |
|---|---|---|
| `coolify-config` | `/data/coolify/` | every 15 min |
| `db-dumps` | `/opt/backrest/db-dumps/` | every 15 min |
| `docker-volumes` | `/var/lib/docker/volumes/` | :05/:20/:35/:50 each hour |

Each server has a pre-backup hook that runs before `coolify-config`. Dumps written vary by server — see each server's repo for the exact dump list:
- Hetzner: `/home/ai/backrest-hetzner/` — Coolify PostgreSQL/Redis, ContextForge SQLite, EmailCleanup SQLite
- Authentik: `/home/ai/backrest-authentik/` — Authentik PostgreSQL, shared coolify-redis
- Compshop: `/home/ai/backrest-compshop/` — two Supabase PostgreSQL instances, coolify-redis

Logical dumps are preferable for database restores because they avoid filesystem-level inconsistency.

## Retention policy (all servers, not enforced by this repo)

96 recent, 168 hourly, 30 daily, 12 weekly.

## Restore levels

| Level | Scope | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| A | Individual files | `restic restore` via Backrest |
| B | Single container volume | Backrest restore → restart container |
| C | Full plan | Backrest restore of entire plan path |
| D | Full repository | Restic restore from DO Spaces |
| E | Full VPS rebuild | Infrastructure runbook + repository restore |

## Request flows

### Authentication

1. SPA calls `/auth/me` on load
2. Primary: Google OAuth via `/auth/google` → `GoogleStrategy`
3. Fallback: local password via `LocalStrategy` — not a dev shortcut, used when Google SSO is down

### Dashboard

1. Frontend bootstraps, parallel-loads health, status, repos, snapshots, models
2. `computeHealth()` fans out to local Backrest, optional Hetzner Backrest, and SSH
3. Results rendered with explicit degraded-state cues — stale cache, SSH down, API unreachable are distinct states, not collapsed into one

### AI chat

1. User submits prompt → `POST /api/chat`
2. AI tool loop runs (up to 10 rounds)
3. Destructive actions returned as proposals to UI
4. User approves or denies each individually
5. Approved actions posted to `POST /api/execute`

### Direct restore / execute

1. User selects action in UI → `POST /api/execute`
2. Server writes audit entry (before)
3. Server executes Backrest restore or allowlisted SSH operation
4. Server writes audit entry (success or failure)
5. Result returned to UI

## Design constraints

- **No database on the monitor.** File-based audit log and health cache keep the monitor lightweight and deployment simple.
- **No build step for the UI.** Single HTML file.
- **Proposals before execution.** AI and UI paths both require explicit operator approval for destructive actions.
- **Allowlist over passthrough.** SSH commands are typed and allowlisted, not freeform.
- **Backend health computation.** Health is computed server-side and returned as a structured document. The browser does not infer health from raw API counts.

## Application-specific restore flows

Some applications need more than a generic volume restore — they require a coordinated stop → wipe → restore → restart sequence. These are implemented as dedicated SSH scripts on the VPS plus matching server routes and UI cards.

### HiClaw / OpenClaw restore

**Why it needs its own flow:** HiClaw runs multiple containers (`hiclaw-controller`, `hiclaw-manager`) that must be stopped before the volume can be safely replaced, and restarted even if the restore fails partway.

**Script:** `/opt/backrest/scripts/restore-hiclaw.sh` on the Hetzner VPS. Runs as root via SSH. Sequence:
1. Reads restic credentials from `/opt/backrest/config/config.json` at runtime — no credentials stored in the repo.
2. Finds the latest `docker-volumes` snapshot via `docker exec backrest restic snapshots`.
3. Stops `hiclaw-controller` and `hiclaw-manager`.
4. Wipes `hiclaw-data/_data` with `find -mindepth 1 -delete` (ensures no stale post-snapshot files survive).
5. Runs `restic restore --overwrite always` via `docker exec backrest` with env vars injected via `-e`.
6. Restarts both containers regardless of restore success or failure.

**SSH operation:** `restore_hiclaw` in `src/ssh.js`, 10-minute timeout.

**Server route:** `POST /api/restore/hiclaw` — audited before and after. Returns the full restore log.

**Status route:** `GET /api/hiclaw/status` — calls `hiclaw_status` SSH operation, parses `docker inspect` output into `[{ name, status }]`.

**UI:** Restore tab → Application Restores section → HiClaw card. Shows live container status, a confirm dialog explaining what will happen, a spinner during restore, and the full script log on completion or failure.

**Adding a similar flow for a new application:**
1. Write `/opt/backrest/scripts/restore-<app>.sh` on the VPS following the same pattern.
2. Add `restore_<app>` and `<app>_status` to `ALLOWED_OPERATIONS` in `src/ssh.js`.
3. Add `GET /api/<app>/status` and `POST /api/restore/<app>` routes in `src/server.js`.
4. Add a card to the Application Restores section in `renderRestoreTab()`.

## Where to make changes

| Change | Start here |
|---|---|
| Health calculation or status semantics | `computeHealth()` in `src/server.js` |
| Add a new backup server | Add SSH client in `src/server.js`, new endpoints `/api/containers/<server>` + `/api/snapshots/<server>`, update `computeHealth()`, update `renderDashboard()` in `public/index.html`, update AI system prompt in `src/ai.js` |
| Backrest response parsing | `extractArray()` + normalization functions in `src/backrest.js` |
| SSH safety / command policy | `ALLOWED_OPERATIONS` + `executeOperation()` in `src/ssh.js` |
| Dashboard and degraded UX | `renderDashboard()` in `public/index.html` |
| Restore modal | `openRestoreModal()` + `confirmRestore()` in `public/index.html` |
| AI behavior and tool exposure | `src/ai.js` |
| Execution entry point + audit | `/api/execute` in `src/server.js` |

# Configuration

All configuration is via environment variables. Copy `.env.template` to `.env` and fill in all required values before starting the stack.

## AI

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | Yes | OpenRouter API key |
| `DEFAULT_MODEL` | No | OpenRouter model ID. Defaults to the value set in `src/ai.js`. Set to a cheaper model (e.g. `openrouter/auto`) during development. |

## Authentication

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` | Yes | Google OAuth2 client ID |
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` | Yes | Google OAuth2 client secret |
| `GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL` | Yes | OAuth callback URL — must exactly match the redirect URI registered in Google Cloud Console |
| `ALLOWED_EMAILS` | Yes | Comma-separated list of Google email addresses permitted to log in |
| `SESSION_SECRET` | Yes | Express session secret — use a long random string, rotate if compromised |

Local auth (`passport-local`) is active alongside Google OAuth. It is not configured via these env vars — credentials are managed separately and should be treated as a real emergency fallback, not a dev shortcut.

## Backrest

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BACKREST_URL` | Yes | URL of the local Backrest API on the DO droplet, e.g. `http://backrest:9898`. This is the read-only monitor-side instance. |
| `BACKREST_HETZNER_URL` | No | URL of the Hetzner-side Backrest API. If unset, Hetzner Backrest is not queried — health data comes from SSH checks and the shared DO Spaces repo only. |

## SSH

All three backup servers use the same key file (`HETZNER_SSH_KEY_PATH`). Only the host and user differ per server.

**Hetzner (required):**

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HETZNER_SSH_HOST` | Yes | Hetzner VPS hostname or IP (Tailscale: 100.66.37.58) |
| `HETZNER_SSH_PORT` | No | SSH port, default 22 |
| `HETZNER_SSH_USER` | Yes | SSH username (`root`) |
| `HETZNER_SSH_KEY_PATH` | Yes | Path to the private key file — shared by all SSH clients |

**Authentik (optional — enables Authentik containers + snapshot panels):**

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AUTHENTIK_SSH_HOST` | No | Authentik server IP (43.173.75.208) |
| `AUTHENTIK_SSH_USER` | No | SSH username, default `ubuntu` |

**Compshop (optional — enables Compshop containers + snapshot panels):**

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `COMPSHOP_SSH_HOST` | No | Compshop server IP (185.194.148.230) |
| `COMPSHOP_SSH_USER` | No | SSH username, default `root` |

If `AUTHENTIK_SSH_HOST` or `COMPSHOP_SSH_HOST` is unset, that server's SSH client is not created and its dashboard panels return `{ notConfigured: true }`. No error is shown.

## DigitalOcean Spaces / restic

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DO_SPACES_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | Spaces credentials (read access to the backup bucket is sufficient) |
| `DO_SPACES_SECRET_KEY` | Yes | Spaces secret key |
| `DO_SPACES_ENDPOINT` | Yes | Spaces endpoint URL, e.g. `https://nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com` |
| `DO_SPACES_BUCKET` | Yes | Bucket name containing the restic repository |
| `RESTIC_PASSWORD` | Yes | Restic repository encryption password — **must match exactly** the password used by the Hetzner-side Backrest |

## App

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PORT` | No | HTTP listen port, default 3000 |
| `WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST` | No | Docker image digest for immutable pinning — see [deployment](deployment.md#immutable-image-pinning) |

## Notes

**`RESTIC_PASSWORD` mismatch** is a common silent failure. If this doesn't match Hetzner's value, the DO-side Backrest can't decrypt snapshots. Health checks show Backrest as unreachable or returning errors, which looks like a monitor problem when the actual issue is credential configuration.

**`BACKREST_HETZNER_URL`** can be omitted if Hetzner's Backrest API isn't exposed over a network path. SSH-based health checks (`ping`, `disk_space`) still work without it. The health model surface slightly more information when both sides are reachable.

# Development

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 20+
- A Backrest instance to point `BACKREST_URL` at, or leave it pointing at an unreachable address — most API routes degrade gracefully and return empty/stale data rather than crashing
- For SSH features: SSH key with access to the Hetzner VPS

## Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/u2giants/backrest-wiz
cd backrest-wiz
npm install
cp .env.template .env
# edit .env — see docs/configuration.md for all variables
```

## Run

```bash
npm run dev     # node --watch src/server.js — restarts on file change
npm start       # node src/server.js
```

The app listens on `PORT` (default 3000). Point a browser at `http://localhost:3000`.

## Test

```bash
node src/test.js
```

45 assertions, no test framework. Covers all four normalization functions (`normalizeSnapshot`, `normalizeOperation`, `normalizeRepo`, `normalizeDashboard`), `extractArray` envelope handling, and SSH `safeShellArg` patterns. No network required. Runs in under a second. Exit code 0 = all pass, exit code 1 = failure with details printed.

Run this before every push. The CI hook in `.githooks/` enforces it on commit.

## Debug

- **`GET /api/audit`** — every executed action logged with params and result, in insertion order
- **`GET /api/health`** — full health document: per-plan freshness, SSH state, cache age, reachability flags
- **Health cache on disk** — written to `HEALTH_CACHE_PATH`. Inspect to see what the last-good-state snapshot was and when it was written.
- **SSH operations** — logged to stdout with operation name, exit code, and stderr on failure
- **AI cost** — set `DEFAULT_MODEL` to a cheap model during development (e.g. `openrouter/auto`) to reduce latency and cost

## Finding credentials at runtime

When a container's password or secret is unknown, read it from the container's environment — Docker stores all env vars set at container creation and they're readable via:

```bash
docker inspect <container-name> --format '{{range .Config.Env}}{{.}}
{{end}}'
```

This is how `restore-hiclaw.sh` retrieves restic credentials from the Backrest config at runtime rather than hardcoding them. The same technique works for any container (postgres passwords, redis auth, API keys set as env vars at deploy time).

For credentials NOT set as env vars (e.g. secrets mounted as files), check `/proc/<pid>/environ` or look inside the container at known config file paths.

## Extending

### Add an SSH operation

1. Add an entry to `ALLOWED_OPERATIONS` in `src/ssh.js`. Key = operation name, value = an object with the command template. Use `safeShellArg()` for any interpolated arguments.
2. Add the handler case to `executeOperation()`.
3. Expose it in `src/server.js` or wire it as an AI tool.
4. Add assertions to `src/test.js` covering the safe-arg behavior for the new operation.

### Add an AI tool

1. Add the tool definition to the `tools` array in `src/ai.js`.
2. Add a handler case to the tool loop.
3. If the tool proposes a destructive action, return it as a proposal object — **do not execute directly.** The UI shows proposals for operator approval before posting to `/api/execute`. Bypassing this breaks the audit trail and the safety model.

### Add an API route

1. Add the route in `src/server.js` behind the `requireAuth` middleware.
2. Call `auditLog({ action, params, result })` for any mutation.
3. Return a consistent shape — existing routes use `{ ok: true, ... }` on success.

## Intentional quirks

- **Single-file SPA.** `public/index.html` has no build step. Edit it directly. The file is large but self-contained.
- **Dual normalization.** The browser still contains defensive fallback field access alongside the server-side normalization layer. Both layers are correct independently. The overlap is a transition artifact from before normalization moved server-side — it will shrink as upstream shapes stabilize.
- **Local auth fallback.** `passport-local` is wired alongside Google OAuth. It is not a dev convenience — it exists for real operational access when Google SSO is unavailable. Do not remove it.
- **Raw SSH `execute()`.** `src/ssh.js` has a raw command path retained for backward compatibility. It is not exposed through any current UI or AI route. Use `executeOperation()` for new work.
- **File-based state.** The health cache and audit log are files on disk, not a database. This is intentional — it keeps the monitor lightweight and the deployment simple.

*** Begin Patch

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Restore-drill for the most critical backup artifact: Coolify's Postgres dump.
+#
+# This is intentionally standalone and secret-free. Provide restic/Spaces credentials
+# in the environment, then run it from any machine with docker + restic installed.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+DUMP_PATH="${DUMP_PATH:-/db-dumps/coolify-db-latest.sql}"
+POSTGRES_IMAGE="${POSTGRES_IMAGE:-postgres:16-alpine}"
+CONTAINER_NAME="${CONTAINER_NAME:-coolify-db-restore-test-$$}"
+
+required() {
+    local name="$1"
+    if [ -z "${!name:-}" ]; then
+        echo "ERROR: $name is required" >&2
+        exit 2
+    fi
+}
+
+required RESTIC_PASSWORD
+required DO_SPACES_ACCESS_KEY
+required DO_SPACES_SECRET_KEY
+required DO_SPACES_ENDPOINT
+required DO_SPACES_BUCKET
+
+command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "ERROR: docker is required" >&2; exit 2; }
+command -v restic >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "ERROR: restic is required" >&2; exit 2; }
+
+export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="$DO_SPACES_ACCESS_KEY"
+export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="$DO_SPACES_SECRET_KEY"
+export RESTIC_REPOSITORY="${RESTIC_REPOSITORY:-s3:${DO_SPACES_ENDPOINT}/${DO_SPACES_BUCKET}/backrest}"
+
+workdir="$(mktemp -d)"
+cleanup() {
+    docker rm -f "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+    rm -rf "$workdir"
+}
+trap cleanup EXIT
+
+echo "Restoring ${DUMP_PATH} from ${RESTIC_REPOSITORY}"
+restic restore latest --target "$workdir" --include "$DUMP_PATH"
+
+dump_file="$(find "$workdir" -path "*${DUMP_PATH}" -type f | head -1)"
+if [ -z "$dump_file" ] || [ ! -s "$dump_file" ]; then
+    echo "ERROR: restored dump not found or empty: ${DUMP_PATH}" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+dump_bytes="$(wc -c <"$dump_file")"
+if [ "$dump_bytes" -lt 1000 ]; then
+    echo "ERROR: restored dump is suspiciously small (${dump_bytes} bytes)" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "Starting throwaway Postgres container ${CONTAINER_NAME}"
+docker run -d --name "$CONTAINER_NAME" \
+    -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=restore-test \
+    -e POSTGRES_USER=postgres \
+    "$POSTGRES_IMAGE" >/dev/null
+
+for _ in $(seq 1 30); do
+    if docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        break
+    fi
+    sleep 1
+done
+docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null
+
+docker cp "$dump_file" "${CONTAINER_NAME}:/tmp/coolify-db-latest.sql"
+docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" psql -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -U postgres -f /tmp/coolify-db-latest.sql >/dev/null
+
+db_count="$(docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" psql -U postgres -Atc "select count(*) from pg_database;" | tr -d '[:space:]')"
+role_count="$(docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" psql -U postgres -Atc "select count(*) from pg_roles;" | tr -d '[:space:]')"
+
+if [ "${db_count:-0}" -lt 2 ] || [ "${role_count:-0}" -lt 2 ]; then
+    echo "ERROR: restore loaded, but sanity counts are too low (databases=${db_count}, roles=${role_count})" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "OK: Coolify DB dump restored into throwaway Postgres (${dump_bytes} bytes, databases=${db_count}, roles=${role_count})."

role_count="$(docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" psql -U postgres -Atc "select count(*) from pg_roles;" | tr -d '[:space:]')"

if [ "${db_count:-0}" -lt 2 ] || [ "${role_count:-0}" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: restore loaded, but sanity counts are too low (databases=${db_count}, roles=${role_count})" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "OK: Coolify DB dump restored into throwaway Postgres (${dump_bytes} bytes, databases=${db_count}, roles=${role_count})."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Restore-drill for the most critical backup artifact: Coolify's Postgres dump.
#
# This is intentionally standalone and secret-free. Provide restic/Spaces credentials
# in the environment, then run it from any machine with docker + restic installed.

set -euo pipefail

DUMP_PATH="${DUMP_PATH:-/db-dumps/coolify-db-latest.sql}"
POSTGRES_IMAGE="${POSTGRES_IMAGE:-postgres:16-alpine}"
CONTAINER_NAME="${CONTAINER_NAME:-coolify-db-restore-test-$$}"

required() {
    local name="$1"
    if [ -z "${!name:-}" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: $name is required" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
}

required RESTIC_PASSWORD
required DO_SPACES_ACCESS_KEY
required DO_SPACES_SECRET_KEY
required DO_SPACES_ENDPOINT
required DO_SPACES_BUCKET

command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "ERROR: docker is required" >&2; exit 2; }
command -v restic >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "ERROR: restic is required" >&2; exit 2; }

export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="$DO_SPACES_ACCESS_KEY"
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="$DO_SPACES_SECRET_KEY"
export RESTIC_REPOSITORY="${RESTIC_REPOSITORY:-s3:${DO_SPACES_ENDPOINT}/${DO_SPACES_BUCKET}/backrest}"

workdir="$(mktemp -d)"
cleanup() {
    docker rm -f "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    rm -rf "$workdir"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

echo "Restoring ${DUMP_PATH} from ${RESTIC_REPOSITORY}"
restic restore latest --path "$DUMP_PATH" --target "$workdir" --include "$DUMP_PATH"

dump_file="$(find "$workdir" -path "*${DUMP_PATH}" -type f | head -1)"
if [ -z "$dump_file" ] || [ ! -s "$dump_file" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: restored dump not found or empty: ${DUMP_PATH}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

dump_bytes="$(wc -c <"$dump_file")"
if [ "$dump_bytes" -lt 1000 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: restored dump is suspiciously small (${dump_bytes} bytes)" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Starting throwaway Postgres container ${CONTAINER_NAME}"
docker run -d --name "$CONTAINER_NAME" \
    -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=restore-test \
    -e POSTGRES_USER=postgres \
    "$POSTGRES_IMAGE" >/dev/null

for _ in $(seq 1 30); do
    if docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        break
    fi
    sleep 1
done
docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null

docker cp "$dump_file" "${CONTAINER_NAME}:/tmp/coolify-db-latest.sql"
docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" psql -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -U postgres -f /tmp/coolify-db-latest.sql >/dev/null

db_count="$(docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" psql -U postgres -Atc "select count(*) from pg_database;" | tr -d '[:space:]')"
role_count="$(docker exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" psql -U postgres -Atc "select count(*) from pg_roles;" | tr -d '[:space:]')"

if [ "${db_count:-0}" -lt 2 ] || [ "${role_count:-0}" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: restore loaded, but sanity counts are too low (databases=${db_count}, roles=${role_count})" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "OK: Coolify DB dump restored into throwaway Postgres (${dump_bytes} bytes, databases=${db_count}, roles=${role_count})."

What I changed in `backrest-wiz`:

- Removed committed build artifact `backrest-wiz-167ced0.tar`.
- Added `.tar` to [.gitignore](C:/repos/backrest-wiz/.gitignore).
- Added [.gitattributes](C:/repos/backrest-wiz/.gitattributes) so shell/docs/config files stay LF-normalized on Windows.
- Tightened [pre-backup.sh](C:/repos/backrest-wiz/hetzner-producer/scripts/pre-backup.sh) so timestamped `coolify-db` dumps are capped to the latest 96 files, matching “24 hours at 15-minute cadence.” The old age-based cleanup remains as a fallback.
- Added the first concrete Phase B2 restore-drill script: [test-restore-coolify-db.sh](C:/repos/backrest-wiz/hetzner-producer/bin/test-restore-coolify-db.sh). It restores `/db-dumps/coolify-db-latest.sql` from restic into a throwaway Postgres container, imports it, sanity-checks database/role counts, and tears down.
- Updated [HANDOFF.md](C:/repos/backrest-wiz/HANDOFF.md), [hetzner-producer/README.md](C:/repos/backrest-wiz/hetzner-producer/README.md), and [BACKUP-MANIFEST.md](C:/repos/backrest-wiz/hetzner-producer/BACKUP-MANIFEST.md) to point at the new restore drill.

- `bash -n` on both producer scripts
- `node src/test.js`: 47 passed, 0 failed
- `git diff --check`: clean
- Simulated prune behavior kept exactly 96 dump files

I did not touch live infrastructure or run the real restore drill because it needs the real restic/DO Spaces credentials. The next real operational step is to run `hetzner-producer/bin/test-restore-coolify-db.sh` with those credentials, then wire it into a scheduled alerting path.
